summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard recording of the November 3 1984 Berkeley show, highlighted by a blistering Feel Like A Stranger → Cumberland and a soaring Jam‑Drums‑Space sequence.
review: The Miller SBD transfer is praised as one of the best‑sounding Dead tapes on the Archive – crisp mids, deep bass that makes sub‑woofers work, and only occasional wobble noted by a few reviewers. Musically the concert shines with an energetic second set, standout renditions of Feel Like A Stranger > Cumberland, It Must Have Been The Roses > The Music Never Stopped, and a powerful Jam‑Drums‑Space excursion, though some listeners felt the latter half wavered.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers (8/10) give 5‑star ratings and laud both the performance and the superb soundboard quality; only a few note minor inconsistencies, keeping the overall score in the high‑four range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix with strong low‑end that ‘works the sub‑woofers’; Miller’s transfer praised as one of the best SBDs on Archive. A couple of reviewers mention occasional wobble or unevenness in parts of the second set.

standout_songs: It Must Have Been The Roses > The Music Never Stopped
show_quality.setlist_flow: A quirky, intimate second‑set sequence that blends classic favorites with newer material; high energy early in set II (Stranger → Cumberland) and a deep‑cut Down In The Bottom that split opinion.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals described as solid throughout; his solo on the second set praised for creativity, especially when sharing mic with Bob.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass highlighted as a ‘bass lover’s dream’ with powerful low‑end; several reviewers note Phil sounds amazing.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir’s voice noted as “shot” by one reviewer but still part of an electrifying performance; his interaction with Jerry during Gloria praised.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland receives consistent praise for sounding great and adding energy to the mix.
band_member_comments.Drums: Jam → Drums → Space segment singled out as a favorite, indicating strong drumming throughout.
